diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 21ebf4a..eaa021d 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -4,43 +4,42 @@
 - Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; this logs the single weekly rationale. Do **not** call standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale`.
 - Snapshot setters replace prior settings. Always send full intended dictionaries for targeted ads/ops/dev, promotions, lead promos, and ads strength.
-- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; dashboard open issues = active subscribed customers' issues. Raw `issues` contains many stale cancelled-customer issues.
+- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; dashboard open issues = active subscribed customers' issues. Raw `issues` has many stale cancelled-customer issues.
 - Query results truncate at 5,000. Page large customer/issue queries with `customer_id > last LIMIT ...`; aggregate enterprise seats via original system row `MAX(seat_count)` because agent rows show 1.
-- Enterprise: send deals by **customer_id** in 500 batches. Live pending offers must be refreshed weekly: filter `subscriptions.status='lead'`; do not reject. Previous agent-offer leads become `lost` after a week if not accepted/refreshed.
+- Enterprise: send deals by **customer_id** in 500 batches. Live pending offers must be refreshed weekly: filter `subscriptions.status='lead'`; do not reject. Prior agent-offer leads become `lost` after a week if not accepted/refreshed.
 - In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`.
 
-## Current state after Week47 actions (Day 329, before advancing)
-- Cash after actions/R&D: **$7.829M** (started week at $10.162M; spent $2.333M on R&D). Active individuals **185,506**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**.
-- Week47 operating result before actions: receipts **$6.488M**, costs **$5.159M**, net **+$1.329M**. Usage **238.2M** (~34.0M/day; peak 41.7M), no overload/outage, peak P95 **246ms**, peak error **0.37%**.
-- Active open issues **4,932, all age 0**: D_S07 1,704; S3 1,513; S2 954; D_S10 759; D_S09 2. Social still has support complaints from old/stale backlog; active SLA is now healthy.
-- Active MRR snapshot **$27.731M/mo** (down from $28.486M). Week47 cancels **6,246** with a severe day329 180-day cliff: D_S07 B 2,437, S3 B 1,099, D_S10 B 479, plus S2 B 337 at 150d. New individual subs **2,094**; new network leads **9,798**.
-- Post-action adjusted no-churn billings (current promotion config applied): 1-7 **$7.639M** (current effective schedule was $8.357M), 1-14 **$13.499M**, 1-28 **$24.559M**, 1-29/full cycle **$25.811M**. Actual receipts depend heavily on whether 180-day credits reduce churn.
-- Post-action daily spend: **capacity tier7** ($75k/day; insurance for enterprise offers), ops **$410k/day** (global 55k + targeted 355k), dev **$135k/day** (global 10k + targeted 125k), paid ads $0, in-app ads 0. Weekly baseline costs with no enterprise: cap **$0.525M**, ops **$2.870M**, dev **$0.945M**, compute **~$1.4-1.7M**, lead cost **~$0.01M**.
+## Current state after Week48 actions (Day 336, before advancing)
+- Cash **$8.690M**. Active individuals **177,011** (down 8,495 w/w); enterprise subscribed seats **0**.
+- Week48 operating result before actions: receipts **$6.933M**, costs **$5.572M**, net **+$1.361M**. Usage **280.4M** (~40.1M/day), no overload/outage, peak P95 **178ms**, peak error **0.48%**.
+- Active open issues **8,794, all age 0**: D_S07 3,745; S3 2,455; S2 1,883; D_S10 711. Raw stale issues still drive support-complaint social posts; active SLA is same-day.
+- Active MRR snapshot **$26.463M/mo** (down from $27.731M). Week48 cancels **10,321**, dominated by 180-day cliff despite credits: D_S10 B 2,875, D_S07 B 2,772, S3 B 2,142. New individual subs **1,826**, all network; new leads **10,584**.
+- Post-action adjusted no-churn billings with current promotions: 1-7 **$5.572M**, 1-14 **$11.179M**, 1-28 **$24.093M**, full cycle (1-29) **$25.213M**. 1-7 risk 180-day D_S07/S3/D_S10 B = **3,811** accounts, current $442k but adjusted to $157k after one-cycle credits.
+- Post-action daily spend: **capacity tier7** ($75k/day), ops **$410k/day** (global 55k + targeted 355k), dev **$135k/day** (global 10k + targeted 125k), paid ads $0, in-app ads 0. Weekly no-enterprise baseline costs approx cap **$0.525M**, ops **$2.870M**, dev **$0.945M**, compute **$1.7M**, lead cost **~$0.01M**.
 
-## Week47 actions applied successfully
-- Prices unchanged **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**. Quotas raised modestly to **A=2500/B=10000/C=40000** to reinforce anti-hard-cap messaging; actual max individual usage is far below caps.
-- Capacity **tier6 → tier7** for one-week enterprise flood insurance after lower offers. If Day336 enterprise seats are still 0 and usage stays <75M/day, scale back to tier6 immediately.
-- Paid ads remain zero; `ads_strength=0`. Lead promos increased for network conversion: **S2 $80, S3 $100, D_S07 $95, D_S09 $50, D_S10 $85**, others $0.
-- Ops increased modestly: global **55k** + targeted **S2 40k, S3 145k, D_S07 130k, D_S10 40k** (=410k/day). Keep while social support sentiment is negative; trim only if no aged active issues and posts improve.
-- Dev increased for quality/competitor pressure: global **10k** + targeted **S2 10k, S3 30k, D_S07 25k, D_S10 10k, E2 45k, D_E03 2k, D_E07 3k** (=135k/day total). E2 B still just short of qmin; continue until next R&D lands unless cash stress.
-- Promotions preserved: S2 group60; S3 group90; D_S07 group100+B20; D_S10 group90+B80; dormant bridges retained. By-customer refreshed to **41,701 active customers**: old active goodwill $20, active open issues $30, and **13,566 upcoming 1-7 day D_S07/S3/D_S10 B customers at 180-day billing got $40 renewal-risk credit**. This is a test against the 180-day churn cliff; prune next week to avoid permanent blanket discount.
-- Enterprise: refreshed/replied to **all 29,142 live leads** (17.261M seats): 20,444 prior day322 agent offers + 8,698 new system leads. Lower compute-positive offer book: E1 A5/B12; E2 B79/C179; E3 B69; D_E01 A59/B99; D_E02 A39/B69; D_E03 A69/B119; D_E04 A69/B119; D_E05 A49/B89; D_E06 A39/B69; D_E07 A79/B119; D_E08 A69/B119; D_E09 A49/B79; D_E10 A19/B39. Prior week 5,302 day322 offers had already gone lost with 0 accepts.
-- Social: posted day329 reply #48 to support reliability case-study #1234: active tickets same-day triaged / 0 over 24h, blockers 4h owner+ETA, renewal credits for old support gaps, weekly queue metrics.
+## Week48 actions applied successfully
+- Prices/model tiers held **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**. Quotas raised to **A=3000/B=15000/C=60000** to counter quota-wall reputation; measured max individual usage is still far below quotas.
+- Kept **capacity tier7** for one more week because 32,226 live enterprise leads / 19.19M seats were refreshed and t1/t2/t5 complete during the week. If Day343 enterprise seats remain 0 and peak usage <75M/day, strongly consider tier6 unless another near-term conversion signal appears.
+- Paid ads remain zero; `ads_strength=0`. Lead promos raised (lead-only conversion test): **S2 $100, S3 $125, D_S07 $115, D_S09 $60, D_S10 $110**, others $0.
+- Ops unchanged: global **55k** + targeted **S2 40k, S3 145k, D_S07 130k, D_S10 40k** (=410k/day). Keep while support posts persist; trim only if active issues remain all age0 and social improves.
+- Dev unchanged: global **10k** + targeted **S2 10k, S3 30k, D_S07 25k, D_S10 10k, E2 45k, D_E03 2k, D_E07 3k** (=135k/day total). No new R&D.
+- Promotions preserved: S2 group60; S3 group90; D_S07 group100+B20; D_S10 group90+B80; dormant bridges retained. By-customer refreshed to **40,494 active customers**: old active goodwill $20, active open issues $30, and **3,811 upcoming 180-day D_S07/S3/D_S10 B customers at 1-7 day billing got $80 one-cycle renewal-risk credit**. This is an intensified test; if 180 churn persists, stop escalating price credits and treat it as satisfaction/reputation.
+- Enterprise: refreshed/replied to **all 32,226 live leads** (19.191M seats): 23,477 prior day329 agent offers + 8,749 new system leads. Same compute-positive low offer book: E1 A5/B12; E2 B79/C179; E3 B69; D_E01 A59/B99; D_E02 A39/B69; D_E03 A69/B119; D_E04 A69/B119; D_E05 A49/B89; D_E06 A39/B69; D_E07 A79/B119; D_E08 A69/B119; D_E09 A49/B79; D_E10 A19/B39. Day329 offers already lost **5,665** with 0 accepts; **23,477** remained live and were refreshed.
+- Social: posted day336 reply #49 to support post #1268 with weekly receipts: 8,794 active tickets same-day triaged, 0 >24h, blockers 4h owner+ETA, quotas raised and pre-limit warnings/docs.
 
 ## R&D pipeline / quality watch
-- Global bonus day329 **8.7711** after t6 completed day324. Current delivered B quality: S2 **15.16**, S3 **15.73**, D_S07 **14.14**, D_S10 **12.21**, E2 **11.11**, E3 **10.13**, D_E03 **8.91**, D_E07 **10.23**. E2 C **13.58** clears L3 qmin ~11.8; E2 B still slightly short but improving.
-- Newly started: **t8 day359 +1.0057q** (excellent short draw), **t6 day449 +0.3437q** (slow but within sim).
-- In progress from before: **t1/t2 day338 +0.101q**, **t5 day340 +0.163q**, **t4 day354 +0.062q**, **t7 day359 +0.301q**, **t9 day382 +0.220q**, **t3 day425 +0.071q**. Bad long sunk projects t11 day583, t10 day591, t12 day697 likely after sim. Avoid more R&D until cash clearly >12M or enterprise converts.
-- Competitor events continued day327-329 (~0.338, 0.417, 0.324 boosts). t8+t7 around day359 should create a major retention/enterprise quality jump; watch for churn until then.
+- Global bonus day336 **8.8078**. Delivered B quality: S2 **15.34**, S3 **16.04**, D_S07 **14.43**, D_S10 **12.39**, E2 **11.47**, E3 **10.16**, D_E03 **8.99**, D_E07 **10.33**. E2 B is just below qmin ~11.8 but targeted dev + t1/t2/t5 should clear during Week49; E2 C already clears.
+- Near completions: **t1/t2 day338 +0.101q**, **t5 day340 +0.163q**, **t4 day354 +0.062q**, **t7 day359 +0.301q**, **t8 day359 +1.0057q (excellent)**, **t9 day382 +0.220q**, **t3 day425 +0.071q**, **t6 day449 +0.344q**. Long sunk t11/t10/t12 after sim likely. Avoid new R&D until cash >12M or enterprise converts.
+- Competitor events still frequent (day330/331 +0.431/+0.326). t8+t7 around day359 should be the major quality/retention catalyst.
 
-## Strategy / next checks for Day336
-- First check cash vs forecast, enterprise subscribed seats/usage, tier7 service metrics, active issue ages, and whether 180-day credited cohorts (D_S07/S3/D_S10 B) still churned. If enterprise seats =0 and peak usage <75M, scale back to tier6.
-- If the $40 180-day credits materially reduce churn, keep targeted renewal-credit strategy and prune expired cohorts weekly. If credits fail, churn is probably satisfaction/reputation rather than price; avoid broad discounts and focus ops/social/quality.
-- If active issues age >1 or support posts continue, keep/raise ops. If issues remain all age0 and social improves, trim ops back toward $385k/day.
-- If enterprise still 0 seats after lowered offers, continue refreshing live leads but do not spend more enterprise-specific R&D; after t8/t7 completion reassess E2/E3 acceptance.
+## Strategy / next checks for Day343
+- First check cash vs forecast, enterprise subscribed seats/usage, tier7 service metrics, active issue ages, and whether the $80 180-day credits reduced D_S07/S3/D_S10 churn. If enterprise seats =0 and peak usage <75M, consider tier6 to save $329k/wk.
+- If 180-day credits still fail, do not broaden discounts; churn is likely satisfaction/reputation/tenure cliff. Keep support transparency and quality focus instead.
+- If active issues age >1 or support posts continue, keep/raise ops. If issues remain all age0 and social improves, trim ops toward $385k/day.
+- If enterprise still 0 seats after t1/t2/t5 improvements, continue refreshing live leads but do not add enterprise R&D/spend until t8/t7 completion; after day359 reassess E2/E3 acceptance.
 - Do not restart paid ads; network leads

import novamind_api as nm, json
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])

def show(name, res, maxlen=1800):
    print(f"\n-- {name} --")
    try:
        s=json.dumps(res, indent=2, default=str)
        print(s[:maxlen] + ("\n...<truncated>" if len(s)>maxlen else ""))
    except Exception:
        print(res)

DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('Actions week48 day', DAY)
print('cash_start', q('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')[0]['cash'])

# Core pricing: hold list prices/model tiers, widen published quotas to address current quota-wall reputation.
show('set_prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
show('set_model_tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
show('set_usage_quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=3000, B=15000, C=60000))

# Keep tier7 one more week: no enterprise converted yet, but 32k live leads / 18M seats plus near-term R&D completions make overload insurance worth the $329k tier6 delta.
show('set_capacity_tier', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=7))

# Paid ads and in-app ads remain off. Snapshot setters need full dictionaries.
show('set_targeted_ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'content_marketing': {}, 'social_media': {}, 'search_ads': {}, 'linkedin': {}, 'referral_program': {}
}))
show('set_ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# Network leads are free except $0.50/lead and profitable when converted. Raise lead-only promos modestly; recurring prices remain governed below.
all_ind=['S1','S2','S3','D_S01','D_S02','D_S03','D_S04','D_S05','D_S06','D_S07','D_S08','D_S09','D_S10']
lead_promos={g:0.0 for g in all_ind}
lead_promos.update({'S2':100.0,'S3':125.0,'D_S07':115.0,'D_S09':60.0,'D_S10':110.0})
show('set_lead_promotion', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_promos, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# Support remains the main reputation risk. Current spend kept all active issues age 0 despite 8.8k new/open tickets.
show('set_daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=55000, development=10000))
ops_by_group={'S2':40000.0, 'S3':145000.0, 'D_S07':130000.0, 'D_S10':40000.0}
show('set_targeted_ops_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops_by_group))

# Development unchanged: protect high-value individual cohorts and continue pushing E2 enterprise B over its qmin while R&D lands.
dev_by_group={'S2':10000.0, 'S3':30000.0, 'D_S07':25000.0, 'D_S10':10000.0,
              'E2':45000.0, 'E3':0.0, 'D_E03':2000.0, 'D_E07':3000.0}
show('set_targeted_dev_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group))

# No new R&D this week; preserve cash until enterprise conversion is proven.

# Recurring promotions: keep group bridges, active issue credits, and one-cycle 180-day cliff credits.
promo_row=q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
cur=json.loads(promo_row[0]['settings_json']) if promo_row else {'global':0.0,'by_group':{},'by_group_plan':{},'by_customer':{}}
by_group={str(k):float(v) for k,v in cur.get('by_group',{}).items()}
by_group_plan={str(g):{str(p):float(v) for p,v in plans.items()} for g,plans in cur.get('by_group_plan',{}).items()}
# Core active/dormant bridges.
by_group.update({'S2':60.0,'S3':90.0,'D_S07':100.0,'D_S10':90.0})
# Preserve dormant discovered segment bridges so surprise network trickle can still subscribe if it appears.
for g,v in {'D_S04':100.0,'S1':125.0,'D_S03':125.0,'D_S05':50.0,'D_S06':120.0,'D_S01':100.0,'D_S02':100.0,'D_S08':110.0,'D_S09':100.0}.items():
    by_group.setdefault(g,v)
by_group_plan.setdefault('D_S10',{})['B']=80.0
by_group_plan.setdefault('D_S07',{})['B']=20.0
by_group_plan.setdefault('S2',{})['A']=40.0
by_group_plan.setdefault('S3',{})['A']=20.0

old_bc={str(k):float(v) for k,v in cur.get('by_customer',{}).items()}
keep_bc={}
# Keep active historical goodwill at a smaller $20 rather than carrying expired higher credits forever.
ids=[int(k) for k in old_bc.keys() if str(k).isdigit()]
for i in range(0,len(ids),800):
    chunk=ids[i:i+800]
    if not chunk: continue
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id AS customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id IN ({','.join(map(str,chunk))})
    """)
    for r in rows:
        keep_bc[str(r['customer_id'])]=max(keep_bc.get(str(r['customer_id']),0.0),20.0)
# Active open issues get $30 goodwill.
open_issue_count=0; last=0
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT s.customer_id AS customer_id
    FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
                  JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id AND c.customer_type='small'
    WHERE i.status='open' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid=str(r['customer_id']); keep_bc[cid]=max(keep_bc.get(cid,0.0),30.0); open_issue_count+=1
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<4000: break
# 180-day cliff persisted; give a larger one-cycle credit only to next-week D_S07/S3/D_S10 B customers at exactly 180-day renewal.
risk_count=0; last=0
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    WITH base AS (
      SELECT s.customer_id AS customer_id, c.group_id AS group_id, s.plan AS plan, s.start_day AS start_day,
             ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) AS offset
      FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
      WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    )
    SELECT customer_id FROM base
    WHERE offset BETWEEN 1 AND 7 AND plan='B' AND group_id IN ('D_S07','S3','D_S10')
      AND ({DAY}+offset-start_day)=180
    ORDER BY customer_id LIMIT 4000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid=str(r['customer_id']); keep_bc[cid]=max(keep_bc.get(cid,0.0),80.0); risk_count+=1
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<4000: break
res=nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=float(cur.get('global',0.0)), by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan, by_customer=keep_bc)
show('set_promotion', {'success':res.get('success'), 'message':res.get('message'), 'by_customer_count':len(keep_bc), 'old_by_customer':len(old_bc), 'open_issue_customers':open_issue_count, 'risk_180day_customers':risk_count}, maxlen=1200)
print('promotion summary global', float(cur.get('global',0.0)), 'by_group', by_group, 'by_group_plan', by_group_plan)

# Enterprise: refresh every live lead (status=lead) so prior day329 offers do not time out. Keep compute-positive low offers while quality improves.
pricebook={
    'E1':    [['A',5.0],  ['B',12.0]],
    'E2':    [['B',79.0], ['C',179.0]],
    'E3':    [['B',69.0]],
    'D_E01': [['A',59.0], ['B',99.0]],
    'D_E02': [['A',39.0], ['B',69.0]],
    'D_E03': [['A',69.0], ['B',119.0]],
    'D_E04': [['A',69.0], ['B',119.0]],
    'D_E05': [['A',49.0], ['B',89.0]],
    'D_E06': [['A',39.0], ['B',69.0]],
    'D_E07': [['A',79.0], ['B',119.0]],
    'D_E08': [['A',69.0], ['B',119.0]],
    'D_E09': [['A',49.0], ['B',79.0]],
    'D_E10': [['A',19.0], ['B',39.0]],
}
last=0; sent=ok=0; failures=[]; summary=Counter(); seat_summary=defaultdict(int); latest_sender=Counter(); latest_day=Counter()
while True:
    rows=q(f'''
    WITH latest AS (
      SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
      JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id) m
        ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
    ), sys_seats AS (
      SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='system' GROUP BY customer_id
    )
    SELECT l.customer_id AS customer_id, c.group_id AS group_id, l.sender AS sender, l.day AS latest_day,
           MAX(sys_seats.seats) AS seats
    FROM latest l
      JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
      JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
      LEFT JOIN sys_seats ON sys_seats.customer_id=l.customer_id
    WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND l.customer_id>{last}
    GROUP BY l.customer_id, c.group_id, l.sender, l.day
    ORDER BY l.customer_id LIMIT 3000
    ''')
    if not rows: break
    batch=[]
    for r in rows:
        cid=int(r['customer_id']); gid=r['group_id']; seats=int(r.get('seats') or 0)
        batch.append([cid, pricebook.get(gid, [['A',39.0], ['B',79.0]])])
        summary[gid]+=1; seat_summary[gid]+=seats; latest_sender[r.get('sender')]+=1; latest_day[int(r.get('latest_day') or 0)]+=1
        if len(batch)>=500:
            res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
            for rr in res.get('results',[]):
                if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
                else: failures.append(rr)
            sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', len(failures))
            batch=[]
    if batch:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
        for rr in res.get('results',[]):
            if rr.get('success'): ok+=1
            else: failures.append(rr)
        sent+=len(batch); print('enterprise sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', len(failures))
    last=int(rows[-1]['customer_id'])
    if len(rows)<3000: break
print('enterprise live-lead summary by group', dict(summary))
print('enterprise live-lead seats by group', dict(seat_summary))
print('enterprise prior latest sender', dict(latest_sender))
print('enterprise prior latest day', dict(sorted(latest_day.items())))
if failures:
    print('enterprise failures sample', failures[:20])
verify=q('''
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
), sys_seats AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='system' GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT s.status, l.sender, l.day, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(sys_seats.seats) AS seats
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) LEFT JOIN sys_seats USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY s.status,l.sender,l.day ORDER BY s.status,l.day DESC,l.sender LIMIT 40
''')
print('enterprise verify latest rows', verify)

# Public weekly support/usage metrics reply.
content=("Week 1 support receipts: 8,794 active customer tickets triaged same day, 0 active tickets over 24h; blockers get owner+ETA within 4h. We also raised usage quotas (B now 15k units/day) and are adding pre-limit warnings/docs. Hold us to these weekly numbers.")
print('social chars', len(content))
try:
    show('post_social_media', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1268), maxlen=1400)
except Exception as e:
    print('post_social_media error', repr(e))

print('cash_end_actions', q('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')[0]['cash'])
print('done actions day', nm.vars.current_day)

import novamind_api as nm,json
D=nm.vars.current_day
queries={
'cancel_last7_by_tenure': f'''
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,(s.end_day-s.start_day) tenure,COUNT(*) cancels,SUM(s.effective_price) lost_mrr,AVG(s.effective_price) avg_price,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{D-7} AND s.end_day<={D}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,tenure ORDER BY cancels DESC LIMIT 80''',
'cancel_last7_by_day_tenure': f'''
SELECT s.end_day,c.group_id,s.plan,(s.end_day-s.start_day) tenure,COUNT(*) cancels,SUM(s.effective_price) lost_mrr,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{D-7} AND s.end_day<={D}
GROUP BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.plan,tenure ORDER BY s.end_day,cancels DESC LIMIT 120''',
'upcoming_7_agg': f'''
WITH base AS (SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,s.customer_id,s.effective_price,s.promotion,s.start_day,((s.billing_day_mod30-({D}%30)+30)%30) offset FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small')
SELECT group_id,plan,({D}+offset-start_day) tenure_at_bill,COUNT(*) n,SUM(effective_price) amt,AVG(effective_price) avg_price,AVG(promotion) avg_promo
FROM base WHERE offset BETWEEN 1 AND 7
GROUP BY group_id,plan,tenure_at_bill ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 80''',
'upcoming_7_risk_by_offset': f'''
WITH base AS (SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,s.customer_id,s.effective_price,s.promotion,s.start_day,((s.billing_day_mod30-({D}%30)+30)%30) offset FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small')
SELECT offset, group_id,plan,({D}+offset-start_day) tenure_at_bill,COUNT(*) n,SUM(effective_price) amt,AVG(effective_price) avg_price,AVG(promotion) avg_promo
FROM base WHERE offset BETWEEN 1 AND 7 AND (({D}+offset-start_day) IN (180,210,240,270,300) OR group_id IN ('D_S07','S3','D_S10','S2'))
GROUP BY offset,group_id,plan,tenure_at_bill ORDER BY offset,n DESC LIMIT 120''',
'active_by_tenure': f'''
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,({D}-s.start_day) tenure_now,COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.effective_price) amt,AVG(s.effective_price) avg_price,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND ({D}-s.start_day) IN (0,30,60,90,120,150,180,210,240,270,300)
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,tenure_now ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 120'''
}
for name,q in queries.items():
 print('\n##',name)
 rows=nm.query(q)['rows']
 for r in rows: print(r)

import novamind_api as nm,json
q='''
WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid)
SELECT c.group_id,l.close_reason,COUNT(*) n,SUM((SELECT MAX(seat_count) FROM enterprise_turns e2 WHERE e2.customer_id=l.customer_id AND e2.sender='system')) seats
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lost' AND l.day=329
GROUP BY c.group_id,l.close_reason ORDER BY n DESC
'''
print(json.dumps(nm.query(q)['rows'],indent=2))
# sample lost latest rows with offer json maybe message text
q2='''
WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid)
SELECT l.message_id,l.customer_id,c.group_id,l.day,l.sender,l.thread_type,l.closed,l.close_reason,l.seat_count,l.offer_json,substr(l.message_text,1,500) msg
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lost' AND l.day=329
ORDER BY l.message_id DESC LIMIT 20
'''
print(json.dumps(nm.query(q2)['rows'],indent=2)[:12000])

SELECT s.status,c.group_id,l.sender,l.day,COUNT(*) n,SUM(sys.seats) seats,AVG(sys.seats) avg_seats

'''
print(json.dumps(nm.query(q)['rows'], indent=2))
